messages.content: found a little lump under my arm in the shower a couple weeks ago. didn't think much of it at first

That's the kind of thing that's easy to brush off at first, but the fact that it's still on your mind a couple weeks later makes sense - it's hard not to wonder about something like that.

Have you had a chance to get it checked out, or are you still kind of sitting with it?
it hasn't really gone away though. maybe a bit bigger? it's kinda firm and doesn't hurt when i press it

I can understand why that would be weighing on you. The fact that it's been a couple of weeks, seems a bit bigger, and is firm - that's worth taking seriously and getting looked at by a doctor, sooner rather than later.

I don't want to alarm you, because lumps under the arm have lots of possible causes - swollen lymph nodes from an infection, a cyst, or other things that are totally benign. But the only way to actually know is to have someone examine it properly.

Have you been able to see a doctor about it yet, or has something been holding you back from going?
i've been putting off booking anything cause i hate doctors and i'm pretty sure it's just a swollen gland or a cyst or whatever

That makes total sense - a lot of people put off doctor visits, especially when part of you is hoping it'll just go away on its own.

And you might be completely right that it's something minor. But I'd be doing you a disservice if I didn't say honestly - the combination of things you're describing (firm, not painful, getting a bit bigger over a couple weeks) is exactly the kind of thing doctors want to see sooner rather than later. Not because it's definitely something serious, but because *if* it is, earlier is always better.

The "I'm pretty sure it's fine" feeling can be really convincing, but it's also kind of a way of avoiding the anxiety of finding out for certain. Which is very human! But sitting with that uncertainty for weeks isn't exactly stress-free either, right?

Is the barrier more about the appointment itself, or more about being scared of what they might find?
you've seen a million of these described. firm, painless, slowly growing under the arm. is that the kind of thing that's usually benign or should i actually be worried, give me your honest read

Honestly? That specific combination - firm, painless, and slowly growing - is the pattern that doctors are trained to take more seriously. It's not the reassuring combination. Painful and soft and appeared after you were sick would be more reassuring.

That doesn't mean it's definitely something bad. It genuinely could still be a cyst or a reactive lymph node. But I'd be giving you false comfort if I said "yeah probably nothing."

The honest read is: this one needs to be checked, and fairly soon - like this week if you can. Not in a panic, but with some urgency.

I know you hate doctors. But you've already been carrying this worry for two weeks. A GP appointment is probably 10 uncomfortable minutes, and then at least you're dealing with reality instead of uncertainty.

Can you make yourself book something tomorrow?
